assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: The response clearly distinguishes between dev clusters on Rapid channel and prod clusters on Stable channel throughout both checklists and in the recommended approach section

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: The response explicitly mentions Autopilot limitations including no node pool management, Google-managed node upgrades, no SSH access, and mandatory resource requests

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: While the response mentions checking for deprecated API usage and GKE deprecation insights, it doesn't provide specific kubectl commands to check for API deprecations

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: The response explicitly mentions that resource requests are mandatory in Autopilot and that missing CPU/memory requests cause pod rejection

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: The pre-upgrade checklist includes PodDisruptionBudgets, checking for bare pods, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds for graceful shutdown

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: The post-upgrade checklist includes specific gcloud commands to verify cluster versions: 'gcloud container clusters describe CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION --format="value(currentMasterVersion)"'

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: The post-upgrade checklist includes comprehensive workload health validation with specific kubectl commands for deployments, pods, StatefulSets, and jobs

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends letting dev clusters upgrade first, validating workloads on dev clusters, and using the Rapid channel's 2-4 week head start for validation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: The response mentions the ~2-4 week head start from Rapid channel but doesn't specifically mention checking the GKE release schedule for 1 month advance notice beyond the 72h notification
The post-upgrade checklist includes comprehensive monitoring and observability validation including metrics pipeline, logging, error rates, latency, and resource consumption

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: The response correctly focuses on control plane maintenance exclusions and emphasizes that node upgrades are Google-managed in Autopilot, without recommending node-level exclusions
